computerReply: Negative carbon. Negative known life components. Substance inorganic.
situationalContext: Dr. Crusher is performing initial tests on the newly discovered inorganic sample in the medical lab.
Data requests a recheck of the analysis after the computer initially identifies the sample as inorganic.
Dr. Crusher asks the computer for the current magnification level while examining the inorganic sample.

computerReply: Not repetitive or sequential. Pattern not recognised.
situationalContext: Dr. Crusher asks the computer to analyze the light flashes emanating from the inorganic sample.
userQuery: Analyse the pattern of the flashes.

computerReply: Unable to specify. Theoretically not possible from this substance.
situationalContext: Dr. Crusher asks the computer to identify the source of the mysterious flashes from the sample.
userQuery: What is the source of the flashes?

situationalContext: Dr. Crusher instructs the computer to theorize about the source of the flashes despite theoretical impossibilities.
userQuery: Disregard incongruity and theorise as to source.

situationalContext: Data requests a spectral analysis of the life form at a specific magnification from the computer.
userQuery: Please show me the spectral analysis magnification twelve K.

situationalContext: Worf asks the computer for a determination on whether the analyzed substance is alive.
